intake.title: Every Living System Pays a Maintenance Cost
intake.subtitle: The invisible work that determines whether living systems merely survive — or become.

overrides.curatorNotes: Systems/biology via structure + information; maintenance as embodied capacity.

connections.rationale: returns to the chamber where this inquiry began.
extends the argument first made in the published volume.
this inquiry also touches embodiment — the lived body as participant in the real.
this inquiry also touches structure — what holds form while everything changes.
this inquiry also touches information — signal, structure, and what travels between minds.
this inquiry also touches consciousness — the texture of awareness itself.
asks the same living question from a different angle: What lies beneath perception.
echoes nearby work — Constraint Is Not the Opposite of Freedom.
echoes nearby work — You Have to Go Far Enough to Make a Loop.
grows from the earlier essay Constraint Is Not the Opposite of Freedom.
